name: mergen
title: Mergen — Agent Execution Governance (AEG)
shortDescription: The inline gate between your AI agent and production. Blocks destructive tool calls in <1ms. HITL approval for schema mutations. Tamper-evident Agent Blunder Log.
longDescription:
Mergen is the first Agent Execution Governance (AEG) platform. It wraps your MCP server as a transparent proxy: before any tool handler executes, Mergen evaluates the call against your local policy engine in under 1ms. Destructive commands (rm -rf, terraform destroy, DROP TABLE) are blocked and logged. Schema mutations are held — the Promise suspends until an operator approves or denies via Slack webhook. Every intercepted action is written to a hash-chained, tamper-evident Agent Blunder Log that your CISO can audit.

Sentry and Datadog tell you after an AI agent has corrupted your database. Mergen is the deterministic gate that prevents it.

The Inside-Out progression: Layer 1 — local execution gate on the developer's machine. Layer 2 — CI/CD safety gate for AI-generated PRs. Layer 3 — Agent IAM with ephemeral credential brokering (coming).

tools.name: triage_incident
tools.summary: Full autonomous loop — diagnosis + optional fix execution.

tools.name: execute_fix
tools.summary: Execute a hypothesis fix (requires confirm: true + passes local gate).

tools.name: analyze_runtime
tools.summary: Causal analysis — root cause + fix hint, no execution.

tools.name: get_recent_logs
tools.summary: Console events from the ring buffer.

tools.name: get_network_activity
tools.summary: HTTP/fetch events with status, duration, body.

tools.name: validate_fix
tools.summary: Compare error counts before/after a fix — records verdict.
